questions.question: Who was the first winner of Survivor?
questions.fun_fact: Richard famously competed naked in the final immunity challenge and won with a 4-3 jury vote over Kelly Wiglesworth.

questions.question: Who won Survivor: Heroes vs. Villains?
questions.fun_fact: Sandra became the first two-time winner in Survivor history, having also won Pearl Islands (Season 7).

questions.question: Who won Survivor: Cagayan?
questions.fun_fact: Tony built a spy shack near the camp water well to eavesdrop on other players conversations.

questions.question: Which winner won by a unanimous jury vote in their first appearance, receiving every single vote?
questions.fun_fact: Earl Cole's unanimous 9-0-0 victory in Fiji was the first unanimous win in Survivor history.

questions.question: Who won the second season of Survivor, The Australian Outback?
questions.fun_fact: Colby famously chose to take Tina to the Final 2 instead of the less-liked Keith Famie, costing himself the win in what he called a move based on integrity.

questions.question: In what season did the mandatory fire-making challenge at Final 4 debut?
options: Season 35: Heroes vs. Healers vs. Hustlers
questions.fun_fact: Starting in Season 35, the final 4 immunity challenge loser could no longer be voted out. The two non-immune, non-saved players competed in fire-making.

questions.question: Which winner is known for the "anybody but me" strategy?
questions.fun_fact: Sandra's strategy of deflecting the target onto anyone else became her signature approach and worked in two separate winning seasons.

questions.question: Who is the youngest person to ever win Survivor?
questions.fun_fact: Jenna Morasca was 21 years old when she won Survivor: The Amazon, making her the youngest winner in show history.

questions.question: In Survivor: Tocantins, who defeated Stephen Fishbach to win the title of Sole Survivor?
questions.fun_fact: J.T. played what many consider the perfect game: winning unanimously after never receiving a single vote against him all season.

questions.question: Who won Survivor: Micronesia - Fans vs. Favorites?
questions.fun_fact: Parvati's Black Widow Brigade alliance is considered one of the most dominant alliances in Survivor history.

questions.question: What was the jury vote count in the Survivor: Borneo finale?
questions.fun_fact: Richard Hatch won 4-3 over Kelly Wiglesworth, with Greg Buis famously asking both finalists to pick a number between 1 and 10 to decide his vote.

questions.question: Who is the oldest person to win Survivor?
questions.fun_fact: Bob Crowley was 57 years old when he won Survivor: Gabon, making him the oldest winner in show history.

questions.question: Who won Survivor: Cambodia - Second Chance?
questions.fun_fact: Jeremy revealed at Final Tribal Council that his wife Val was pregnant, which moved several jurors and contributed to his unanimous 10-0-0 win.

questions.question: Which winner was voted out on Day 8 and returned from Edge of Extinction to win?
questions.fun_fact: Chris Underwood was voted out on Day 8, returned on Day 35, and won the game 4 days later, making it the most controversial win in Survivor history.

questions.question: Which female winner won 4 individual immunity challenges during her winning season?
questions.fun_fact: Kim Spradlin dominated One World both strategically and physically in what many call one of the most dominant winning games ever.

questions.question: Who won Survivor: David vs. Goliath?
questions.fun_fact: Nick created named alliances with almost every player in the game, including The Rockstars, The Mason-Dixon Line, and The Thoroughbreds.

questions.question: Who won Survivor: Samoa despite being overshadowed by Russell Hantz's edit?
questions.fun_fact: Natalie's under-the-radar social game defeated Russell Hantz's aggressive strategic game 7-2, proving social bonds often trump flashy moves.

questions.question: Which winner gave up immunity at the Final 4 Tribal Council and made fire to earn their way to FTC?
questions.fun_fact: Chris gave up immunity and made fire against Rick Devens, a bold move that impressed the jury and helped him win the title.

questions.question: Who won Survivor: Ghost Island in the first-ever jury vote tiebreaker?
questions.fun_fact: Wendell and Domenick tied 5-5, with Laurel casting the deciding vote for Wendell. It is the only time a jury vote has gone to a tiebreaker.

questions.question: Which Survivor winner had previously been on The Amazing Race before winning Survivor?
questions.fun_fact: Natalie Anderson and her twin sister Nadiya competed on The Amazing Race Season 21 before Natalie won Survivor: San Juan del Sur.

questions.question: On which season did Boston Rob Mariano finally win Survivor on his fourth attempt?
questions.fun_fact: Rob played in Marquesas, All-Stars, Heroes vs. Villains, and finally won Redemption Island, controlling the game from start to finish.

questions.question: Who won Survivor: Kaoh Rong (Season 32)?
questions.fun_fact: Michele's win over fan-favorite Aubry sparked one of the biggest debates in Survivor history about whether social play or strategic play should be more valued.

questions.question: Who won Survivor: Palau?
questions.fun_fact: Tom Westman, a NYC firefighter, won 5 individual immunity challenges and dominated both the physical and strategic game.

questions.question: Who fell into the campfire in Survivor: The Australian Outback?
questions.fun_fact: Michael Skupin passed out and fell hands-first into the fire on Day 17, resulting in severe burns and the first medical evacuation in Survivor history.

questions.question: Who told the "dead grandmother" lie in Survivor: Pearl Islands?
questions.fun_fact: Jonny Fairplay's friend Thunder D visited during the loved ones visit and confirmed his grandmother had died. She was actually alive and in on the scheme.

questions.question: Which castaway gave up individual immunity and was immediately voted out in Micronesia?
questions.fun_fact: Erik was convinced by Cirie, Natalie, Amanda, and Parvati to give up the immunity necklace. Jeff Probst called it one of the dumbest moves in Survivor history.

questions.question: Who gave the famous "Rats and Snakes" speech at the first-ever Final Tribal Council?
questions.fun_fact: Sue Hawk's speech comparing Kelly to a rat and Richard to a snake is considered the most iconic jury speech in Survivor history.

questions.question: Which castaway was famously voted out with two hidden immunity idols in their pocket?
questions.fun_fact: James Clement was voted out at Final 7 in China while holding two hidden immunity idols, one of the biggest blunders in Survivor history.

questions.question: In which season did the famous mutiny occur where Candice and Penner switched tribes?
questions.fun_fact: Candice and Penner mutinied from Aitu to Raro, but the remaining Aitu Four (Yul, Ozzy, Becky, Sundra) went on one of the greatest underdog runs in Survivor history.

questions.question: Who was the first person to be eliminated from Survivor?
questions.fun_fact: Sonja Christopher, a 63-year-old retired musician, was the first person voted out of Survivor. She tripped during the first immunity challenge.

questions.question: What iconic prop did Rupert Boneham wear throughout Pearl Islands?
questions.fun_fact: Rupert's tie-dye shirt became so iconic that it was essentially his brand. He was so beloved by fans that he won a  million viewer vote during All-Stars.

questions.question: In which season did Coach Wade tell his famous story about being captured by Amazonian natives?
questions.fun_fact: Coach's story about being kidnapped while kayaking was met with extreme skepticism by his tribemates, particularly Stephen Fishbach and J.T. Thomas.

questions.question: What did Russell Hantz do to sabotage his tribe in Samoa?
questions.fun_fact: Russell dumped water from canteens, burned socks, and hid the machete to create chaos at camp while forming alliances with everyone.

questions.question: In Heroes vs. Villains, what costly mistake did Tyson make that led to his own elimination?
options: Switched his vote against the agreed-upon plan
Played his idol on the wrong person
questions.fun_fact: Tyson changed his vote from Russell to Parvati, splitting the vote incorrectly and allowing Russell to idol out Tyson. It is considered one of the worst strategic blunders ever.

questions.question: Which season featured the Outcasts twist, where previously voted-out players returned?
questions.fun_fact: Burton Roberts and Lill Morris returned via the Outcast twist. It was so controversial that producers did not repeat the exact format for years.

questions.question: Who sat out of the final immunity challenge in Borneo, confident he would be taken to the end?
questions.fun_fact: Richard deliberately stepped off during the Final 3 immunity challenge, knowing whoever won would take him. Kelly won and took Richard, proving his read correct.

questions.question: Which two players quit simultaneously in Survivor: Nicaragua?
questions.fun_fact: NaOnka and Purple Kelly quit at the same Tribal Council, the only time two players have quit simultaneously. Both were still allowed on the jury.

questions.question: In which season did a player famously say "It's a f***ing stick!" about a fake idol?
questions.fun_fact: Eliza Orlins said this when Jason Siska gave her the fake idol Ozzy had carved from a stick. She played it anyway in desperation, and Jeff confirmed it was not a real idol.

questions.question: Who won the  million America's Tribal Council fan vote in All-Stars?
questions.fun_fact: Rupert Boneham was so popular with viewers that he won the additional  million fan vote during the All-Stars reunion, separate from the game prize.

questions.question: Which castaway was blindsided in one of the most emotional Tribal Councils ever during Game Changers?
questions.fun_fact: Cirie was eliminated at the Final 6 without receiving a single vote, as all other players were immune through idols and advantages. It is called "Advantagegeddon."

questions.question: In which season did Jenna Morasca quit to be with her sick mother?
questions.fun_fact: Jenna's mother was battling cancer, and Jenna felt compelled to leave the game. Her mother passed away just 8 days after Jenna returned home.

questions.question: What was the name of the chicken that became a mascot on Survivor: China?
options: No chicken was a mascot in China
questions.fun_fact: The famous Survivor chicken was actually Mark the Chicken from Kaoh Rong (Season 32), who Tai Trang bonded with and kept as a pet, refusing to let the tribe eat him.

questions.question: Who said "I'm against you, Russell" during the Samoa Final Tribal Council?
questions.fun_fact: Natalie's firm stance against Russell at FTC was part of her quiet social game that contrasted sharply with Russell's aggressive gameplay and contributed to her win.

questions.question: Which Tribal Council featured the first-ever joint tribal where two tribes voted together?
questions.fun_fact: In Game Changers, Mana and Nuku attended tribal together. Malcolm Freberg was blindsided when the other tribe targeted him, unable to defend himself.

questions.question: Who collapsed from heat exhaustion during a challenge in Kaoh Rong, requiring emergency medical care?
questions.fun_fact: Caleb Reynolds was airlifted out after collapsing during a grueling reward challenge in extreme heat. Two other players also needed medical attention at the same time.

questions.question: In which season were hidden immunity idols first introduced?
questions.fun_fact: Gary Hogeboom found and played the first hidden immunity idol in Guatemala, setting a precedent for every future season.

questions.question: Who played two hidden immunity idols at the same Tribal Council in Heroes vs. Villains?
questions.fun_fact: Parvati played one idol on Jerri and one on Sandra at the merge Tribal Council, blindsiding JT's alliance in one of the most iconic idol plays ever.

questions.question: What is the "idol nullifier" and in which season was it first used?
options: A fake idol planted by production, Season 35
questions.fun_fact: Carl Boudreaux used the idol nullifier in David vs. Goliath to cancel Dan Rengering's idol play, sending Dan home in a shocking blindside.

questions.question: Who found hidden immunity idols without any clues, changing how players searched for them?
questions.fun_fact: Russell Hantz found idols without clues in both Samoa and Heroes vs. Villains, fundamentally changing how players searched for idols in future seasons.

questions.question: In Survivor: Cagayan, what was Tony Vlachos's special idol nicknamed?
questions.fun_fact: Named after Tyler Perry who reportedly suggested the idea, this idol could be played AFTER votes were read. Tony never had to use it.

questions.question: What was unique about Yul Kwon's hidden immunity idol in Cook Islands?
options: It could be played after votes were read
It was hidden on Exile Island
It was valid through the Final 3
questions.fun_fact: Yul's God Idol could be played after votes were read and was valid through Final 3, making it perhaps the most powerful idol ever. He used it as a strategic threat without actually playing it.

questions.question: Ozzy Lusth carved a fake idol out of what material in Micronesia?
questions.fun_fact: Ozzy carved a face into a stick and wrapped it in string. Jason Siska found it and believed it was a real idol, playing it at Tribal Council to his embarrassment.

questions.question: How many votes did Kelley Wentworth negate with her idol play at the Final 9 in Cambodia?
questions.fun_fact: All 9 other players voted for Kelley, but her idol negated every single vote. Andrew Savage was eliminated on the revote, leading to his shocked reaction.

questions.question: In which season did three hidden immunity idols get played at a single Tribal Council?
questions.fun_fact: At the Final 6 in Game Changers, Tai played two idols and Troyzan played one, leaving only Sarah (with immunity) safe through normal means.

questions.question: In which season did Ben Driebergen find and play idols at three consecutive Tribal Councils?
options: Season 35: Heroes vs. Healers vs. Hustlers
questions.fun_fact: Ben found idols at the Final 7, 6, and 5 Tribal Councils, saving himself each time when he was the obvious target.

questions.question: What happened to the idol that JT gave to Russell in Heroes vs. Villains?
options: Russell gave it to Parvati who played it on someone
questions.fun_fact: JT wrote Russell a letter with the idol, believing Russell was in danger. Russell gave it to Parvati, who used it at the merge to blindside JT's own alliance.

questions.question: Who found a hidden immunity idol during a challenge while no one was looking?
questions.fun_fact: During a challenge in Cambodia, Kelley grabbed an idol from the underside of the challenge platform while no one was looking, one of the most clutch idol finds ever.

questions.question: What is a "Super Idol" as seen in Kaoh Rong?
options: An idol that gives you two extra votes
Two idol halves combined into one that can be played after votes are read
An idol that lasts for two Tribal Councils
An idol that lets you steal someone's vote
questions.fun_fact: In Kaoh Rong, two regular idol halves could be combined into a Super Idol playable after the vote. Tai had one half but refused to combine it with Scot's.

questions.question: In David vs. Goliath, who played a hidden immunity idol on Christian Hubicki to save him?
questions.fun_fact: Davie played his idol on Christian, saving him from what would have been a blindside. It was a key moment that shifted the game dynamics.

questions.question: Who was the first person to play a hidden immunity idol correctly on someone else?
questions.fun_fact: Russell's idol play for Parvati in Samoa set a precedent for playing idols on others as a strategic move.

questions.question: In the split vote strategy, why does an alliance divide their votes between two people?
options: To flush or neutralize a potential idol play
questions.fun_fact: Split voting became essential strategy as hidden immunity idols became more common. An alliance splits votes so the target still goes home even if they play an idol.

questions.question: Who found two hidden immunity idols in China, only to go home holding both?
questions.fun_fact: James was given both idols by Todd as part of his alliance strategy, but was blindsided before he could use either one.

questions.question: In which season did a contestant find an idol clue during a reward feast hidden in their napkin?
questions.fun_fact: In China, idol clues were hidden in elaborately decorated items given to contestants during rewards, requiring sharp observation skills.

questions.question: How many hidden immunity idols did Russell Hantz find across Samoa and Heroes vs. Villains?
questions.fun_fact: Russell found 5 total idols across his two most famous seasons, revolutionizing the way players approach idol hunting.

questions.question: What was the "Three Amigos" idol play in Caramoan?
options: Three people each played an idol on themselves at the same Tribal
An alliance named after a movie
questions.fun_fact: Malcolm played two idols (one on himself, one on Eddie) and Reynold played his own. All three were safe, and they redirected votes to eliminate Phillip Sheppard.

questions.question: Who found a hidden idol inside a coconut on Ghost Island?
questions.fun_fact: Ghost Island featured cursed items from previous seasons that could be "reversed." Many hidden advantages and idols were tied to famous Survivor moments.

questions.question: Hidden immunity idols must be played at what point during Tribal Council?
options: After the vote but before Jeff reads them
questions.fun_fact: Jeff always asks "if anyone has a hidden immunity idol and would like to play it, now would be the time to do so" after votes are cast but before reading them.

questions.question: Who holds the record for most individual immunity challenge wins in a single season (Seasons 1-39)?
questions.fun_fact: Terry Deitz won 5 individual immunity challenges in Panama, keeping himself safe when he was consistently targeted post-merge.

questions.question: Which castaway is widely considered the greatest challenge competitor in Survivor history?
questions.fun_fact: Ozzy dominated physical challenges across four seasons (Cook Islands, Micronesia, South Pacific, Game Changers) and holds the record for most career immunity wins.

questions.question: In which season did Joe Anglim first appear and go on his impressive immunity run?
questions.fun_fact: Joe won multiple individual immunities in Worlds Apart and was voted out immediately after losing immunity, showing how much of a threat he was.

questions.question: Who famously stood on a buoy for nearly 12 hours in the final immunity challenge of Palau?
questions.fun_fact: Ian eventually stepped down and asked to be voted out, sacrificing the game for his friendship with Tom. It is one of the most emotional moments in Survivor history.

questions.question: Which female contestant holds the record for most individual immunity wins in a single season (tied)?
questions.fun_fact: Both Kim Spradlin (One World) and Chrissy Hofbeck (HHH) won 4 individual immunities, sharing the single-season record for women.

questions.question: What type of challenge is the Survivor Auction?
options: A reward challenge where players bid on items
questions.fun_fact: Strategic players often save all their money for a game advantage. The auction has been a fan-favorite recurring event throughout Survivor history.

questions.question: Tom Westman won how many individual immunity challenges in Palau?
questions.fun_fact: Tom's 5 individual immunity wins helped him dominate the post-merge game and he is widely considered one of the best all-around winners.

questions.question: Which challenge involves contestants laying under a metal grate as the tide rises?
questions.fun_fact: Last Gasp requires breathing through smaller and smaller air gaps as water rises. It is considered one of Survivor's most psychologically intense challenges.

questions.question: In the classic Loved Ones challenge, what do players compete for?
options: Time with a visiting family member or friend
An extra vote at Tribal Council
A clue to a hidden idol
questions.fun_fact: The Loved Ones challenge is consistently one of the most emotional moments each season, often bringing even the toughest players to tears.

questions.question: Which season first introduced Exile Island?
questions.fun_fact: Exile Island sent one player to a desolate island between challenges, where they could search for hidden immunity idol clues. It was named in the season's subtitle.

questions.question: Which season had the most medical evacuations during challenges and gameplay?
questions.fun_fact: Kaoh Rong had 3 medical evacuations: Caleb Reynolds (heat exhaustion), Neal Gottlieb (infected knee), and Joe del Campo (intestinal blockage).

questions.question: What was the "Gross Food Challenge" in early Survivor seasons?
options: Eating local delicacies like balut, grubs, and tarantulas
Eating challenge for most food in 5 minutes
questions.fun_fact: The Gross Food Challenge featured balut (fertilized duck eggs), tarantulas, sea slugs, and live grubs. It was a fan-favorite recurring challenge in early seasons.

questions.question: Who won the most individual immunity challenges across all their Survivor appearances?
questions.fun_fact: Ozzy's challenge dominance across four seasons gives him the most career individual immunity wins in Survivor history.

questions.question: What is the "Hands on a Hard Idol" endurance challenge?
options: Players keep one hand on a wooden totem; last to let go wins
Players hold onto a rope over water
Players balance on a narrow beam
Players hold a bucket of water overhead
questions.fun_fact: This endurance challenge has appeared in multiple seasons and is one of the show's most grueling tests of willpower.

questions.question: Which contestant won individual immunity at age 57, the oldest winner of an immunity challenge?
questions.fun_fact: Bob Crowley won 3 individual immunity challenges in Gabon and went on to become the oldest Sole Survivor in the show's history.

questions.question: Which season featured a tribe that lost every single pre-merge immunity challenge?
questions.fun_fact: The Ulong tribe in Palau lost every immunity challenge, being reduced to just Stephenie LaGrossa, who was absorbed into the Koror tribe.

questions.question: Who is known for his signature "Ozzy necklace" of individual immunity wins across multiple seasons?
questions.fun_fact: Ozzy's immunity necklace became almost synonymous with his name, as he so frequently won individual immunity in every season he played.

questions.question: In which season did the final immunity challenge famously involve standing on narrow poles in the ocean?
questions.fun_fact: The Palau finale challenge lasted almost 12 hours between Tom and Ian standing on buoys, making it the longest challenge in Survivor history.

questions.question: What is the record for most consecutive individual immunity wins in a single season?
questions.fun_fact: Terry Deitz won 5 consecutive individual immunity challenges in Panama, the longest winning streak in Survivor history through Season 39.

questions.question: Which player won 4 individual immunity challenges in Worlds Apart?
questions.fun_fact: Mike Holloway went on an immunity run in the final stretch of Worlds Apart, saving himself from elimination and winning the game.

questions.question: Kelly Wiglesworth won how many of the last 5 individual immunity challenges in Borneo?
questions.fun_fact: Kelly's immunity run kept her safe during the endgame, though she ultimately lost to Richard Hatch in the Final Tribal Council 4-3.

questions.question: What is a "blindside" in Survivor?
options: When a contestant is voted out unexpectedly
questions.fun_fact: The term blindside has become so popular in Survivor that it is now commonly used in everyday language to describe unexpected betrayals.

questions.question: Who created the first formal voting alliance in Survivor?
questions.fun_fact: Richard formed the Tagi Four with Sue, Rudy, and Kelly. It was the first formal alliance in Survivor history and forever changed the game.

questions.question: What strategy did Cirie Fields use to convince Erik to give up immunity in Micronesia?
options: She told him it would redeem his character with the jury
questions.fun_fact: Cirie masterminded the plan by having Natalie tell Erik that giving up immunity would prove his honor to the jury. It is considered her finest strategic moment.

questions.question: What is the "Pagonging" strategy?
options: Systematically eliminating the opposing tribe after the merge
A voting pattern to split votes
questions.fun_fact: Named after the Pagong tribe from Borneo, who were systematically voted out one by one by the Tagi alliance after the merge.

questions.question: Who executed the Black Widow Brigade strategy in Micronesia?
questions.fun_fact: The Black Widow Brigade systematically eliminated the male players using charm, manipulation, and strategic blindsides. It is considered one of the most dominant alliances ever.

questions.question: What was Boston Rob's "buddy system" in Redemption Island?
options: No one could talk to outsiders without an ally present
questions.fun_fact: Rob prevented any side deals or flipping by ensuring no one in his alliance could talk to outsiders without a buddy present, giving him total control of information.

questions.question: Who made the controversial move of voting out their own mother in Blood vs. Water?
questions.fun_fact: Ciera voted out her mother Laura at Tribal Council to prove loyalty to her alliance. Jeff Probst has cited it as one of the boldest moves in Survivor history.

questions.question: In Game Changers, what happened to Cirie Fields at the Final 6?
options: She was eliminated without a single vote against her due to advantages
questions.fun_fact: Every other player at the Final 6 was immune through idols or advantages, so Cirie was eliminated by default without receiving a vote. Fans call it Advantagegeddon.

questions.question: Who coined the phrase "voting blocs" to describe fluid alliances?
questions.fun_fact: Stephen popularized voting blocs in Cambodia, where players form temporary alliances for single votes rather than committing to one rigid alliance.

questions.question: Who is credited with introducing the "meat shield" strategy?
questions.fun_fact: Jeremy kept bigger threats like Joe and Spencer in the game ahead of him so they would be targeted first, calling them his meat shields.

questions.question: In which season did "Stick to the plan!" accidentally blow up an alliance's strategy at Tribal?
questions.fun_fact: Keith Nale blurted out "Stick to the plan!" at Tribal Council, accidentally revealing his alliance was planning a blindside and nearly blowing up his own game.

questions.question: What is "Survivor jury management"?
options: Playing to ensure jury votes at Final Tribal
questions.fun_fact: Jury management is voting people out while maintaining enough goodwill that they will vote for you at the end. Kim Spradlin and Earl Cole are considered masters.

questions.question: What strategic advantage is the "Extra Vote"?
options: Cast two votes at one Tribal Council
Vote at a Tribal you are not attending
questions.fun_fact: The Extra Vote was first introduced in Cambodia (Season 31), allowing a player to cast two votes at a single Tribal Council.

questions.question: Who orchestrated the "Three Amigos" triple idol play in Caramoan?
questions.fun_fact: Malcolm played two idols (one on himself, one on Eddie), and Reynold played his own. All three were safe and they redirected votes to eliminate Phillip Sheppard.

questions.question: What is the "Coconut Bandits" alliance?
options: Tyson and Gervase sneaking coconuts in Blood vs. Water
questions.fun_fact: Tyson and Gervase secretly ate extra coconuts while everyone else was starving, bonding over their covert snacking.

questions.question: What does it mean when a player is called a "goat" in Survivor?
options: They are a non-threat brought to the end because they cannot win
They are the leader of an alliance
questions.fun_fact: Being a goat is usually negative in Survivor, meaning someone is dragged to Final Tribal because they have no chance of winning, like Phillip Sheppard or Will Sims.

questions.question: Who orchestrated the blindside of Marcus in Gabon, flipping the game?
questions.fun_fact: Sugar played a chaotic but influential game in Gabon, using her idol and emotional gameplay to repeatedly shift the balance of power.

questions.question: What strategic innovation did Tony Vlachos bring to Cagayan with his "spy shack"?
options: He eavesdropped on camp conversations from a hidden shelter
questions.fun_fact: Tony built a hidden shelter near the water well to listen to private conversations, gaining crucial intelligence about other players' plans.

questions.question: In which season did someone use a "vote steal" advantage for the first time?
questions.fun_fact: The vote steal was first found by Stephen Fishbach in Cambodia but he was voted out the same tribal he used it. Adam Klein used it more successfully in MvGX.

questions.question: What does "playing from the bottom" mean in Survivor?
options: Being in the minority alliance and trying to survive
questions.fun_fact: Players like Chris Daugherty (Vanuatu) and Danni Boatwright (Guatemala) are famous for playing from the bottom and winning despite being outnumbered.

questions.question: Who flipped at the merge in Cook Islands, only to have the Aitu Four come back and dominate?
questions.fun_fact: Candice and Penner mutinied to the larger Raro tribe, but the Aitu Four overcame the numbers disadvantage in one of Survivor's greatest underdog stories.

questions.question: What is a "split tribal" or "double tribal council"?
options: When two tribes go to Tribal together and vote out one person
When two people are voted out in one episode
When Tribal Council is held at camp
questions.fun_fact: Joint tribals became a recurring twist, most famously used in Game Changers when two tribes voted together and blindsided Malcolm Freberg.

questions.question: Where was the first season of Survivor filmed?
questions.fun_fact: Pulau Tiga is a small island off the coast of Malaysian Borneo. The filming beach became a tourist attraction after the show aired.

questions.question: What was the theme of Season 28: Cagayan?
questions.fun_fact: Cagayan is widely considered one of the best seasons in Survivor history, featuring Tony Vlachos and some of the most unpredictable Tribal Councils ever.

questions.question: Which season was the first to feature any returning players?
questions.fun_fact: Guatemala featured Bobby Jon and Stephenie returning from Palau, making it the first season with returning players. All-Stars was the first all-returnee season.

questions.question: In which country was Survivor: Africa filmed?
questions.fun_fact: Season 3 was filmed in the Shaba National Reserve in Kenya, where contestants dealt with lions, elephants, and extreme heat.

questions.question: What controversial twist divided tribes by ethnicity in Season 13: Cook Islands?
options: Tribes divided by ethnicity into four groups
questions.fun_fact: Cook Islands divided tribes into African-American, Asian-American, Hispanic, and Caucasian groups. The twist was dropped after the first swap.

questions.question: Which season featured the "Medallion of Power" twist?
questions.fun_fact: The Medallion of Power gave one tribe a challenge advantage but was so unpopular that it was abandoned partway through the season.

questions.question: What was the twist in Survivor: One World?
options: Both tribes lived on the same beach
Players could swap tribes at will
Players competed individually from day one
questions.fun_fact: In One World, both tribes shared a single beach, creating unique social dynamics as players interacted with the opposing tribe daily.

questions.question: What was the Redemption Island twist?
options: An island where voted-out players dueled to re-enter the game
An island where tribes swap members
questions.fun_fact: Redemption Island appeared in Seasons 22, 23, and 27. Voted-out players competed in duels, with the winner staying for a chance to re-enter.

questions.question: What was the theme of Season 39: Island of the Idols?
options: Two Survivor legends served as mentors on a separate island
Players competed on an island filled with hidden idols
questions.fun_fact: Boston Rob and Sandra lived on the Island of the Idols, where visiting players were taught skills and offered tests in exchange for advantages.

questions.question: Which two seasons were filmed back-to-back with Russell Hantz cast on the second before the first aired?
questions.fun_fact: Samoa and Heroes vs. Villains were filmed back-to-back. Russell was cast on HvV before Samoa aired, so players had no idea about his gameplay.

questions.question: In which season was Edge of Extinction introduced?
questions.fun_fact: Edge of Extinction allowed voted-out players to stay on a desolate island and compete for a chance to re-enter. Chris Underwood used it to win the season.

questions.question: What twist debuted in Season 3: Africa?
questions.fun_fact: The Africa tribe swap was the first time players were forced to switch tribes, completely upending alliance dynamics. Silas Gaither was the first swap victim.

questions.question: In which season were tribes divided into Heroes and Villains?
questions.fun_fact: Heroes vs. Villains is consistently rated the greatest season of Survivor by fans and features an all-star cast sorted by their reputations.

questions.question: How many days does a standard season of Survivor last (Seasons 1-39)?
questions.fun_fact: For Seasons 1-39, the standard length was 39 days. Starting with Season 41, the game was shortened to 26 days.

questions.question: In what year did the first season of Survivor premiere?
questions.fun_fact: Survivor: Borneo premiered on May 31, 2000, and became a cultural phenomenon. The finale drew over 51 million viewers.

questions.question: What was the original show that Survivor was based on?
questions.fun_fact: Survivor was based on the Swedish show Expedition Robinson, which premiered in 1997. Mark Burnett adapted the format for US audiences.

questions.question: Which season had the most contestants at the start (20 players)?
questions.fun_fact: Cook Islands started with 20 contestants divided into four tribes of five, the most in any season through Season 39.

questions.question: What were the names of the two original tribes in Survivor: Borneo?
questions.fun_fact: Tagi (yellow) and Pagong (orange) merged to form Rattana. The Pagong tribe became famous for being systematically eliminated after the merge.

questions.question: Which Survivor season was subtitled Blood vs. Water and featured returning players with their loved ones?
questions.fun_fact: Season 27 featured returning players with loved ones. Season 29 used the same Blood vs. Water format but with all new players.

questions.question: Which season was set in the Pearl Islands of Panama and featured a pirate theme?
questions.fun_fact: Pearl Islands featured a pirate theme with tribes raiding a local village for supplies. It introduced fan-favorites Rupert Boneham and Jonny Fairplay.

questions.question: Which season first used the "Fans vs. Favorites" format?
questions.fun_fact: Micronesia pitted 10 new fans against 10 returning favorites. The format was used again in Caramoan (Season 26). Both were won by Favorites.

questions.question: Survivor moved to a permanent filming location in Fiji starting with which season?
questions.fun_fact: After filming in Fiji once for Season 14, Survivor returned permanently starting with Season 33 due to favorable production conditions and tax incentives.

questions.question: Who was the first two-time winner of Survivor?
questions.fun_fact: Sandra won Pearl Islands (Season 7) and Heroes vs. Villains (Season 20), proving her anybody-but-me strategy works across eras.

questions.question: Who was the first person to play a hidden immunity idol?
questions.fun_fact: Gary Hogeboom (who went by Gary Hawkins to hide his NFL career) played the first hidden immunity idol in Guatemala.

questions.question: Who holds the record for most days played across all Survivor seasons (through Season 39)?
questions.fun_fact: Ozzy has played 128 total days across his four seasons, the most in Survivor history through Season 39.

questions.question: Who was the first person to be medically evacuated from Survivor?
questions.fun_fact: Michael Skupin fell into the campfire in The Australian Outback, resulting in the show's first medevac on Day 17.

questions.question: Who was the first contestant to win without ever receiving a vote against them?
questions.fun_fact: J.T. Thomas played a perfect game in Tocantins: no votes against, won final immunity, and received every jury vote unanimously.

questions.question: Who was the first unanimous winner (receiving all jury votes)?
questions.fun_fact: Earl Cole won Fiji 9-0-0 over Dreamz and Cassandra, the first unanimous jury vote in Survivor history.

questions.question: What was the first season to have a Final 3 instead of a Final 2?
questions.fun_fact: Cook Islands introduced the Final 3 format, which became the standard for most subsequent seasons and changed endgame strategy significantly.

questions.question: Who was the first person to quit Survivor?
questions.fun_fact: Osten asked to be voted out in Pearl Islands. Jeff did not snuff his torch traditionally, instead simply telling him to go.

questions.question: Who was the first player voted out first in two separate seasons?
questions.fun_fact: Francesca was the first boot in both Redemption Island (Season 22) and Caramoan (Season 26), the only player to be first out twice.

questions.question: Who was the first person to return from Redemption Island and re-enter the main game?
questions.fun_fact: Matt won numerous duels on Redemption Island and returned to the game, only to be immediately sent back by Boston Rob's alliance.

questions.question: How many seasons featured the Fans vs. Favorites format (through Season 39)?
questions.fun_fact: Fans vs. Favorites appeared in Season 16 (Micronesia) and Season 26 (Caramoan). Both were won by Favorites: Parvati and Cochran.

questions.question: What is the record for most votes negated by a single idol play?
questions.fun_fact: Kelley Wentworth's idol play at the Final 9 in Cambodia negated 9 votes, the most ever negated by a single idol play.

questions.question: Who was the first person to find a hidden immunity idol on Exile Island?
questions.fun_fact: Terry found the idol on Exile Island in Panama and kept it as insurance throughout the post-merge, though he never played it since he kept winning immunity.

questions.question: Who was the first person voted out while holding a valid hidden immunity idol?
questions.fun_fact: James went home with two idols in his pocket during China, setting the standard for worst idol misplays in Survivor history.

questions.question: Which winner was the first to also win the fan favorite (Sprint Player of the Season) award?
questions.fun_fact: Yul won both Sole Survivor and the fan favorite award in Cook Islands, reflecting his popularity as a strategic and likeable player.

questions.question: Who was the first person voted out of Survivor: All-Stars (the first all-returnee season)?
questions.fun_fact: Tina Wesson, the Season 2 winner, was the first person voted out of All-Stars, showing that previous winners were immediate targets.

questions.question: What is the closest jury vote in Survivor history (through Season 39)?
options: 5-5 tie broken by third finalist (Ghost Island)
questions.fun_fact: Several seasons had 4-3 votes (Borneo, Australian Outback, Marquesas) and Ghost Island had the first-ever 5-5 tie broken by Laurel's vote.

questions.question: Who holds the record for appearing in the most seasons of Survivor (through Season 39)?
questions.fun_fact: Both Ozzy (13, 16, 23, 34) and Boston Rob (4, 8, 20, 22) played 4 times through Season 39. Rob finally won on his fourth attempt.

questions.question: What is the fewest days a winner spent in the main game (excluding Edge of Extinction time)?
questions.fun_fact: Chris Underwood was voted out Day 8 and returned Day 35, spending only about 12 active game days. Most of his 39-day experience was on Edge of Extinction.

questions.question: Which season was the first where the winner was decided by a jury of more than 7 people?
questions.fun_fact: Cook Islands introduced the Final 3, which also expanded the jury. As seasons progressed, jury sizes grew larger with some exceeding 10 members.

questions.question: How many Survivor winners through Season 39 won unanimously (every jury vote)?
questions.fun_fact: Through Season 39, three winners won unanimously: Earl Cole (Fiji, 9-0-0), J.T. Thomas (Tocantins, 7-0), and Jeremy Collins (Cambodia, 10-0-0).

questions.question: Who was the first African-American winner of Survivor?
questions.fun_fact: Vecepia Towery won Survivor: Marquesas (Season 4), becoming the first African-American Sole Survivor.

questions.question: What is the record for most tribal councils attended in a single season?
questions.fun_fact: Some players in later seasons with large casts attended upward of 15-16 tribal councils in a single season, far more than early seasons.

questions.question: Who holds the record for most career challenge wins (individual and team combined)?
questions.fun_fact: Ozzy's four seasons of play gave him the most total challenge wins of any player, combining individual immunities, tribal immunities, and reward wins.

questions.question: Who was the first Survivor contestant to be voted out and later return in the same season?
questions.fun_fact: Burton and Lill both returned via the Outcast twist in Pearl Islands, the first time voted-out players re-entered the game in the same season.
